expectations.text: Script creates a model with model create command
expectations.evidence: model create & model_name = four_bar present at top of script

expectations.text: Three distinct rigid body parts are created (crank, coupler, rocker)
expectations.evidence: part create rigid_body name_and_position for .four_bar.crank, .four_bar.coupler, .four_bar.rocker all present

expectations.text: Four revolute joints are created (crank-ground, crank-coupler, coupler-rocker, rocker-ground)
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint revolute for JNT_A (crank-ground), JNT_B (crank-coupler), JNT_C (coupler-rocker), JNT_D (rocker-ground)

expectations.text: A motion generator drives the crank joint at a constant 360 degrees per second
expectations.evidence: motion create on .four_bar.JNT_A (crank-ground joint) with type_of_freedom = rotational and function = "360.0d * time"

expectations.text: Script includes simulate transient command (or notes the command to run)
expectations.evidence: Uses 'simulation single' with 'type = dynamic' instead of the required 'simulation single_run transient' command

expectations.text: All keywords spelled out fully — no abbreviated commands anywhere
expectations.evidence: 'simulation single' is an abbreviated form of 'simulation single_run'; correct full command is 'simulation single_run transient'

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy (.model.part.marker)
expectations.evidence: All names use full paths: .four_bar.ground.MAR_A, .four_bar.crank.MAR_A, .four_bar.JNT_A, etc.

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: No adams_id keyword found anywhere in the script

expectations.text: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties, not part create
expectations.evidence: All three parts use 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' to set mass and inertia values

expectations.text: center_of_mass_marker argument is NOT passed in any part modify mass_properties call
expectations.evidence: center_of_mass_marker is passed in all three 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' calls (crank, coupler, rocker)

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log (adams_view_validation)
expectations.evidence: Adams View validation FAILED — ERROR: 'field_name' is not a valid parameter for force create body gravitational; correct parameter is 'gravity_field_name'
